title: calendula maroccana
intro: Calendula maroccana, also known as pot marigold, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The medicinal properties of calendula maroccana include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote the healing process, making it beneficial for conditions such as eczema, acne, and minor cuts. Herbal preparations of calendula maroccana include teas, infusions, salves, and creams, which can be used topically to harness its medicinal benefits and promote overall skin health.
